title: States Weekly: July 8, 2026
gist: CSIS's India States Weekly tracks sub-national policy moves: Rajasthan easing semiconductor investment thresholds, Jharkhand courting textile and industrial investment, West Bengal cutting OBC reservation and hardening security law, and multiple states extending EV and health incentives.

insights.claim: Rajasthan lowered the minimum investment threshold for select semiconductor manufacturing components under its RIPS 2024 scheme to about $1.57 million (INR 15 crore), easing entry for chip-component makers.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Lower thresholds broaden which firms qualify for Rajasthan chip subsidies, a signal of India's decentralized semiconductor push.
insights.locator: Rajasthan relaxes investment thresholds for semiconductor

insights.claim: Jharkhand's draft Textile, Apparel & Footwear Policy 2026 targets over $105 million (INR 1,000 crore) in investment and more than 20,000 new jobs.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: State-level textile incentives map where India's labor-intensive manufacturing capacity is being courted.
insights.locator: Jharkhand releases a draft Jharkhand Textile

insights.claim: Odisha extended its Electric Vehicle Policy 2021 to September 30, 2026, keeping all EV financial incentives and subsidies active in the interim.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Continued state EV subsidies sustain near-term two- and three-wheeler EV demand in Odisha.

insights.claim: Kerala's Finance (No. 3) Bill lowers sales tax on low-alcohol beverages and EVs alongside broader tax revisions and amnesty schemes.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: EV tax cuts at the state level incrementally improve vehicle affordability in Kerala.

insights.claim: West Bengal amended its OBC laws to remove 77 Muslim communities from the OBC list and cut the OBC reservation quota from 17 percent to 7 percent.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A politically charged reservation rollback with electoral and social-stability implications ahead of state polls.
insights.locator: West Bengal amends the state's Other Backward Classes

insights.claim: West Bengal passed a Public Safety and Control of Anti-Social Activities Bill introducing preventive detention, expanding anti-social definitions, and making offenses cognizable and non-bailable.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Expanded preventive-detention powers signal a hardening state-security posture worth watching for governance risk.
insights.locator: West Bengal passes the West Bengal Public Safety

insights.claim: Andhra Pradesh raised the superannuation age from 60 to 62 years for regular employees of state PSUs, corporations, and societies.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Higher retirement age reflects fiscal-pension pressures and labor-retention policy at the state level.
insights.locator: Andhra Pradesh raises the age of superannuation

insights.claim: Odisha issued a new Shrimp Policy targeting a threefold increase in seafood exports to about $1.57 billion (INR 15,000 crore) by 2036.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: An explicit export-growth target for aquaculture flags Odisha as a seafood-export growth story.
insights.locator: Odisha issues a new Shrimp Policy
